1	"""
2	Reference code for LLaMA-3.1 training and inference.
3	Will save the model weights into files, to be read from C as initialization.
4	
5	This code differs from GPT-2 very slightly, there are three main differences:
6	1) RoPE: LLaMA uses a different positional encoding scheme called Relative Positional Encoding (RoPE).
7	2) GQA: Grouped Query Attention (GQA) is used to reduce the number of attention heads.
8	3) SwiGLU: Swish-Gated Linear Unit (SwiGLU) is used as the activation function in the MLP.
9	
10	References:
11	# 1) https://github.com/meta-llama/llama-models/blob/main/models/llama3_1/api/tokenizer.py
12	# 2) https://github.com/meta-llama/llama-models/blob/main/models/llama3_1/api/model.py
13	# 3) https://github.com/meta-llama/llama3/blob/11817d47e1ba7a4959b025eb1ca308572e0e3963/llama/generation.py
14	
15	Example launches to only benchmark the speed of bfloat16 compiled GPU training:
16	TODO: add the actual commands
17	"""
18	
19	import argparse
20	import os
21	import math
22	import glob
23	import inspect
24	from contextlib import nullcontext
25	from dataclasses import dataclass
26	from pathlib import Path
27	import time
28	from typing import (
29	    AbstractSet,
30	    Collection,
31	    Dict,
32	    Iterator,
33	    List,
34	    Literal,
35	    Optional,
36	    Sequence,
37	    Tuple,
38	    Union,
39	    cast,
40	)
41	
42	import numpy as np
43	import torch
44	import torch.nn as nn
45	from torch.nn import functional as F
46	import torch._inductor.config as config
47	from torch.nn.parallel import DistributedDataParallel as DDP
48	from torch.distributed import init_process_group, destroy_process_group
49	from torch.distributed.optim import ZeroRedundancyOptimizer
50	import torch.distributed as dist
51	
52	import tiktoken
53	from tiktoken.load import load_tiktoken_bpe
54	
55	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
56	# PyTorch nn.Module definitions for the LLaMA 3.x model
57	
58	# Used in Grouped Query Attention (GQA), broadcasts the key and value tensors
59	def repeat_kv(x: torch.Tensor, n_rep: int) -> torch.Tensor:
60	    """torch.repeat_interleave(x, dim=2, repeats=n_rep)"""
61	    bs, slen, n_kv_heads, head_dim = x.shape
62	    if n_rep == 1:
63	        return x
64	    return (
65	        x[:, :, :, None, :]
66	        .expand(bs, slen, n_kv_heads, n_rep, head_dim)
67	        .reshape(bs, slen, n_kv_heads * n_rep, head_dim)
68	    )
69	
70	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
71	# RoPE related
72	
73	def reshape_for_broadcast(freqs_cis: torch.Tensor, x: torch.Tensor):
74	    ndim = x.ndim
75	    assert 0 <= 1 < ndim
76	    assert freqs_cis.shape == (x.shape[1], x.shape[-1])
77	    shape = [d if i == 1 or i == ndim - 1 else 1 for i, d in enumerate(x.shape)]
78	    return freqs_cis.view(*shape)
79	
80	def apply_scaling(freqs: torch.Tensor):
81	    # Values obtained from grid search
82	    scale_factor = 8
83	    low_freq_factor = 1
84	    high_freq_factor = 4
85	    old_context_len = 8192  # original llama3 length
86	
87	    low_freq_wavelen = old_context_len / low_freq_factor
88	    high_freq_wavelen = old_context_len / high_freq_factor
89	    new_freqs = []
90	    for freq in freqs:
91	        wavelen = 2 * math.pi / freq
92	        if wavelen < high_freq_wavelen:
93	            new_freqs.append(freq)
94	        elif wavelen > low_freq_wavelen:
95	            new_freqs.append(freq / scale_factor)
96	        else:
97	            assert low_freq_wavelen != high_freq_wavelen
98	            smooth = (old_context_len / wavelen - low_freq_factor) / (
99	                high_freq_factor - low_freq_factor
100	            )
101	            new_freqs.append((1 - smooth) * freq / scale_factor + smooth * freq)
102	    return torch.tensor(new_freqs, dtype=freqs.dtype, device=freqs.device)
103	
104	def apply_rotary_emb(
105	    xq: torch.Tensor,
106	    xk: torch.Tensor,
107	    freqs_cis: torch.Tensor,
108	) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
109	    xq_ = torch.view_as_complex(xq.float().reshape(*xq.shape[:-1], -1, 2))
110	    xk_ = torch.view_as_complex(xk.float().reshape(*xk.shape[:-1], -1, 2))
111	    freqs_cis = reshape_for_broadcast(freqs_cis, xq_)
112	    xq_out = torch.view_as_real(xq_ * freqs_cis).flatten(3)
113	    xk_out = torch.view_as_real(xk_ * freqs_cis).flatten(3)
114	    return xq_out.type_as(xq), xk_out.type_as(xk)
115	
116	def precompute_freqs_cis(
117	    dim: int, end: int, theta: float = 10000.0, use_scaled: bool = False
118	):
119	    freqs = 1.0 / (theta ** (torch.arange(0, dim, 2)[: (dim // 2)].float() / dim))
120	    t = torch.arange(end, device=freqs.device, dtype=torch.float32)
121	    if use_scaled:
122	        freqs = apply_scaling(freqs)
123	    freqs = torch.outer(t, freqs)
124	    freqs_cis = torch.polar(torch.ones_like(freqs), freqs)  # complex64
125	    return freqs_cis
126	
127	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
128	# LLaMA building blocks
129	
130	# LLaMA reference code explicitly implemented RMSNorm so we copy pasted it
131	# (https://github.com/meta-llama/llama-models/blob/main/models/llama3_1/api/model.py)
132	# we could also use nn.RMSNorm, it has slightly different numeric properties, but equivalent
133	class RMSNorm(torch.nn.Module):
134	    def __init__(self, dim: int, eps: float = 1e-6):
135	        super().__init__()
136	        self.eps = eps
137	        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(dim))
138	
139	    def _norm(self, x):
140	        return x * torch.rsqrt(x.pow(2).mean(-1, keepdim=True) + self.eps)
141	
142	    def forward(self, x):
143	        output = self._norm(x.float()).type_as(x)
144	        return output * self.weight
145	
146	class CausalSelfAttention(nn.Module):
147	
148	    def __init__(self, config):
149	        super().__init__()
150	        assert config.n_embd % config.n_head == 0
151	
152	        self.n_head = config.n_head
153	        self.n_kv_head = config.n_kv_head
154	        self.n_rep = self.n_head // self.n_kv_head
155	        self.hd = config.n_embd // config.n_head
156	        self.use_kv = config.use_kv
157	        self.flash = config.flash
158	
159	        self.c_attn = nn.Linear(config.n_embd, (config.n_head + 2 * config.n_kv_head) * self.hd, bias=False)  # key, query, value projections
160	        self.c_proj = nn.Linear(config.n_embd, config.n_embd, bias=False)  # output projection
161	
162	        # static KV cache - we could alternatively allocate it outside of the model and just pass it in when needed
163	        if self.use_kv:
164	            self.cache_k = torch.zeros((config.max_gen_batch_size, config.block_size, config.n_kv_head, self.hd))
165	            self.cache_v = torch.zeros((config.max_gen_batch_size, config.block_size, config.n_kv_head, self.hd))
166	
167	    def forward(self, x, freqs_cis=None, start_pos=None, mask=None):
168	        B, T, C = x.size() # batch size, sequence length, embedding dimensionality (n_embd)
169	        # calculate query, key, values for all heads in batch and move head forward to be the batch dim
170	        qkv = self.c_attn(x)
171	        q, k, v = qkv.split([self.n_head * self.hd, self.n_kv_head * self.hd, self.n_kv_head * self.hd], dim=-1)
172	        q, k, v = map(lambda t: t.view(B, T, -1, self.hd), (q, k, v))  # (B, T, NH, HD)
173	
174	        q, k = apply_rotary_emb(q, k, freqs_cis=freqs_cis)  # rotate QK (rope)  <-- 1. difference compared to GPT-2
175	
176	        if self.use_kv and not self.training and start_pos >= 0:  # use kv-caching during inference
177	            self.cache_k[:B, start_pos : start_pos + T] = k
178	            self.cache_v[:B, start_pos : start_pos + T] = v
179	            k = self.cache_k[:B, : start_pos + T]
180	            v = self.cache_v[:B, : start_pos + T]
181	
182	        k = repeat_kv(k, self.n_rep)  # GQA <-- 2. difference compared to GPT-2
183	        v = repeat_kv(v, self.n_rep)
184	
185	        q, k, v = map(lambda t: t.transpose(1, 2), (q, k, v))  # (B, NH, T, HD)
186	
187	        if self.flash:
188	            # flashattention
189	            # if T == 1 no need to mask, otherwise the function complains
190	            # scaled_dot_product_attention expects a mask where value of True indicates that the element should take part in attention
191	            # our mask is the opposite, so we need to invert it
192	            y = F.scaled_dot_product_attention(q, k, v, mask == 0 if T > 1 else None)
193	        else:
194	            # manual implementation of attention
195	            # this materializes the large (T,T) matrix for all the queries and keys
196	            scores = (q @ k.transpose(-2, -1)) * (1.0 / math.sqrt(self.hd))
197	            if mask is not None:
198	                scores.masked_fill_(mask, torch.finfo(scores.dtype).min)
199	            att = F.softmax(scores.float(), dim=-1).type_as(q)
200	            y = att @ v # (B, NH, T, T) x (B, NH, T, HD) -> (B, NH, T, HD)
201	        y = y.transpose(1, 2).contiguous().view(B, T, C)
202	        y = self.c_proj(y)
203	        return y
204	
205	class MLP(nn.Module):
206	
207	    def __init__(self, config):
208	        super().__init__()
209	        hidden_dim = 4 * config.n_embd
210	        hidden_dim = int(2 * hidden_dim / 3)
211	        # custom dim factor multiplier
212	        if config.ffn_dim_multiplier is not None:
213	            hidden_dim = int(config.ffn_dim_multiplier * hidden_dim)
214	        hidden_dim = config.multiple_of * ((hidden_dim + config.multiple_of - 1) // config.multiple_of)
215	        self.c_fc = nn.Linear(config.n_embd, hidden_dim, bias=False)
216	        self.c_fc2 = nn.Linear(config.n_embd, hidden_dim, bias=False)
217	        self.c_proj = nn.Linear(hidden_dim, config.n_embd, bias=False)
218	
219	    def forward(self, x):
220	        # SwiGLU self.c_proj(F.silu(self.c_fc2(x)) * self.c_fc(x))  <-- 3. difference compared to GPT-2
221	        x1 = self.c_fc(x)
222	        x2 = self.c_fc2(x)
223	        x2 = F.silu(x2)
224	        x = x1 * x2
225	        x = self.c_proj(x)
226	        return x
227	
228	class Block(nn.Module):
229	
230	    def __init__(self, config):
231	        super().__init__()
232	        self.ln_1 = RMSNorm(config.n_embd, config.norm_eps)
233	        self.attn = CausalSelfAttention(config)
234	        self.ln_2 = RMSNorm(config.n_embd, config.norm_eps)
235	        self.mlp = MLP(config)
236	
237	    def forward(self, x, freqs_cis=None, start_pos=None, mask=None):
238	        x = x + self.attn(self.ln_1(x), freqs_cis, start_pos, mask)
239	        x = x + self.mlp(self.ln_2(x))
240	        return x
241	
242	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
243	# The main LLaMA 3.1 model
244	
245	@dataclass
246	class LlamaConfig:
247	    version: str = "3.1"
248	    block_size: int = 8192
249	    vocab_size: int = 128256
250	    n_layer: int = 32
251	    n_head: int = 32
252	    n_kv_head: int = 8
253	    n_embd: int = 4096
254	    ffn_dim_multiplier: float = 1.3
255	    multiple_of: int = 1024
256	    norm_eps: float = 1e-5
257	    rope_theta: float = 500000.0
258	    use_scaled_rope: bool = True
259	    max_gen_batch_size: int = 4
260	    use_kv: bool = True
261	    flash: bool = False  # use flashattention?
262	
263	    def __init__(self, **kwargs):
264	        for k, v in kwargs.items():
265	            if hasattr(self, k):
266	                setattr(self, k, v)
267	        assert self.n_kv_head <= self.n_head
268	        assert self.n_head % self.n_kv_head == 0
269	        assert self.n_embd % self.n_head == 0
270	
271	class LLaMA(nn.Module):
272	
273	    def __init__(self, config):
274	        super().__init__()
275	        self.config = config
276	
277	        self.transformer = nn.ModuleDict(dict(
278	            wte = nn.Embedding(config.vocab_size, config.n_embd),
279	            h = nn.ModuleList([Block(config) for _ in range(config.n_layer)]),
280	            ln_f = RMSNorm(config.n_embd, config.norm_eps),
281	        ))
282	        self.lm_head = nn.Linear(config.n_embd, config.vocab_size, bias=False)
283	
284	        # init all weights, use a torch rng object to be very careful
285	        self.init_rng = torch.Generator()
286	        self.init_rng.manual_seed(42)
287	
288	        self.freqs_cis = precompute_freqs_cis(
289	            config.n_embd // config.n_head,
290	            config.block_size * 2,
291	            config.rope_theta,
292	            config.use_scaled_rope,
293	        )
294	
295	    def forward(self, idx, targets=None, return_logits=True, start_pos=0):
296	        _, t = idx.size()
297	        assert t <= self.config.block_size, f"Cannot forward sequence of length {t}, block size is only {self.config.block_size}"
298	
299	        # forward the LLaMA model itself
300	        x = self.transformer.wte(idx) # token embeddings of shape (b, t, n_embd)
301	        freqs_cis = self.freqs_cis[start_pos:start_pos+t]
302	
303	        mask = torch.triu(torch.ones((t, t), device=next(self.parameters()).device, dtype=torch.bool), diagonal=1)
304	
305	        for i, block in enumerate(self.transformer.h):
306	            x = block(x, freqs_cis, start_pos, mask)
307	        x = self.transformer.ln_f(x)
308	
309	        if targets is not None:
310	            # if we are given some desired targets also calculate the loss
311	            logits = self.lm_head(x).float()
312	            loss = F.cross_entropy(logits.view(-1, logits.size(-1)), targets.view(-1), ignore_index=-1)
313	        else:
314	            # inference-time mini-optimization: only forward the lm_head on the very last position
315	            logits = self.lm_head(x[:, [-1], :]).float() # note: using list [-1] to preserve the time dim
316	            loss = None
317	
318	        # there are performance reasons why not returning logits is prudent, if not needed
319	        if not return_logits:
320	            logits = None
321	
322	        return logits, loss
323	
324	    @staticmethod
325	    def adapt_llama_state_dict_keys(checkpoint, config: LlamaConfig):
326	        # Modify key names from Meta's LLaMA to our LLaMA
327	        # our key names are derived from GPT-2's key names
328	        checkpoint['transformer.wte.weight'] = checkpoint.pop('tok_embeddings.weight')
329	
330	        for i in range(config.n_layer):
331	            for name in ['attention_norm', 'ffn_norm']:
332	                old_key = f'layers.{i}.{name}.weight'  # e.g. layers.x.attention_norm.weight -> transformer.h.x.ln_1.weight
333	                new_key = f'transformer.h.{i}.ln_{1 if name == "attention_norm" else 2}.weight'
334	                checkpoint[new_key] = checkpoint.pop(old_key)
335	
336	        for i in range(config.n_layer):
337	            for name in ['attention.wq', 'attention.wk', 'attention.wv']:
338	                old_key = f'layers.{i}.{name}.weight'
339	                new_key = f'transformer.h.{i}.attn.c_attn.weight'
340	                if name == 'attention.wq':
341	                    checkpoint[new_key] = checkpoint.pop(old_key)
342	                else:  # merge 3 weights into transformer.h.x.attn.c_attn.weight
343	                    checkpoint[new_key] = torch.cat((checkpoint[new_key], checkpoint.pop(old_key)), dim=0)
344	            old_key = f'layers.{i}.attention.wo.weight'
345	            new_key = f'transformer.h.{i}.attn.c_proj.weight'
346	            checkpoint[new_key] = checkpoint.pop(old_key)
347	
348	        ffn_map = {'w1': 'c_fc2', 'w2': 'c_proj', 'w3': 'c_fc'}
349	        for i in range(config.n_layer):
350	            for name in ['feed_forward.w1', 'feed_forward.w2', 'feed_forward.w3']:
351	                old_key = f'layers.{i}.{name}.weight'
352	                new_key = f'transformer.h.{i}.mlp.{ffn_map[name.split(".")[-1]]}.weight'
353	                checkpoint[new_key] = checkpoint.pop(old_key)
354	
355	        checkpoint['transformer.ln_f.weight'] = checkpoint.pop('norm.weight')
356	        checkpoint['lm_head.weight'] = checkpoint.pop('output.weight')
357	
358	        return checkpoint
359	
360	    @staticmethod
361	    def adapt_llama_state_dict_keys_hf(checkpoint, config: LlamaConfig):
362	        # Modify key names from HuggingFace's LLaMA to our LLaMA
363	        # our key names are derived from GPT-2's key names
364	        checkpoint['transformer.wte.weight'] = checkpoint.pop('model.embed_tokens.weight')
365	
366	        # We need to unpermute K and V because HF script permuted the original Meta-LLaMA weights
367	        # see: https://github.com/huggingface/transformers/blob/main/src/transformers/models/llama/convert_llama_weights_to_hf.py
368	        def unpermute(w, n_heads, dim1, dim2):
369	            return w.view(n_heads, 2, dim1 // n_heads // 2, dim2).transpose(1, 2).reshape(dim1, dim2)
370	
371	        for i in range(config.n_layer):
372	            for name in ['input_layernorm', 'post_attention_layernorm']:
373	                old_key = f'model.layers.{i}.{name}.weight'  # e.g. layers.x.attention_norm.weight -> transformer.h.x.ln_1.weight
374	                new_key = f'transformer.h.{i}.ln_{1 if name == "input_layernorm" else 2}.weight'
375	                checkpoint[new_key] = checkpoint.pop(old_key)
376	
377	        for i in range(config.n_layer):
378	            for name in ['self_attn.q_proj', 'self_attn.k_proj', 'self_attn.v_proj']:
379	                old_key = f'model.layers.{i}.{name}.weight'
380	                new_key = f'transformer.h.{i}.attn.c_attn.weight'
381	                if name == 'self_attn.q_proj':
382	                    checkpoint[new_key] = unpermute(checkpoint.pop(old_key), config.n_head, config.n_embd, config.n_embd)
383	                else:  # merge 3 weights into transformer.h.x.attn.c_attn.weight
384	                    tensor = checkpoint.pop(old_key)
385	                    if name == 'self_attn.k_proj':
386	                        tensor = unpermute(tensor, config.n_kv_head, config.n_kv_head * (config.n_embd // config.n_head), config.n_embd)
387	                    checkpoint[new_key] = torch.cat((checkpoint[new_key], tensor), dim=0)
388	            old_key = f'model.layers.{i}.self_attn.o_proj.weight'
389	            new_key = f'transformer.h.{i}.attn.c_proj.weight'
390	            checkpoint[new_key] = checkpoint.pop(old_key)
391	
392	        ffn_map = {'gate_proj': 'c_fc2', 'down_proj': 'c_proj', 'up_proj': 'c_fc'}
393	        for i in range(config.n_layer):
394	            for name in ['gate_proj', 'down_proj', 'up_proj']:
395	                old_key = f'model.layers.{i}.mlp.{name}.weight'
396	                new_key = f'transformer.h.{i}.mlp.{ffn_map[name]}.weight'
397	                checkpoint[new_key] = checkpoint.pop(old_key)
398	
399	        checkpoint['transformer.ln_f.weight'] = checkpoint.pop('model.norm.weight')
400	
401	        return checkpoint
402	
403	    @classmethod
404	    def from_pretrained_llama3_hf(cls, model_id):
405	        """Loads pretrained LLaMA model weights from HuggingFace"""
406	        from transformers import AutoModelForCausalLM, AutoTokenizer
407	        assert model_id == "meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3.1-8B", "Only the 8B-base model is supported for now"
408	        model_args = LlamaConfig()
409	
410	        model = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained(model_id)
411	        checkpoint = LLaMA.adapt_llama_state_dict_keys_hf(model.state_dict(), model_args)
412	
413	        original_default_type = torch.get_default_dtype()  # save the default type
414	        torch.set_default_tensor_type(torch.cuda.BFloat16Tensor)  # much faster loading
415	        model = LLaMA(model_args)
416	        model.load_state_dict(checkpoint, strict=False)
417	        torch.set_default_tensor_type(torch.tensor([], dtype=original_default_type, device="cpu").type())  # restore default type
418	
419	        tokenizer = AutoTokenizer.from_pretrained(model_id)
420	        tokenizer.pad_id = 128004  # this is the pad token id for LLaMA 3.1 base, we need to set this explicitly as our generate func expects it
421	        tokenizer.stop_tokens = [tokenizer.eos_token_id]
422	        model.tokenizer = tokenizer
423	        return model
424	
425	    @classmethod
426	    def from_pretrained_llama3_meta(cls, ckpt_dir, tokenizer_path):
427	        """Loads pretrained LLaMA model weights from a checkpoint directory"""
428	        model_args = LlamaConfig()
429	
430	        ckpt_path = sorted(Path(ckpt_dir).glob("*.pth"))[0]
431	        checkpoint = torch.load(ckpt_path, map_location="cpu", weights_only=True)
432	        checkpoint = LLaMA.adapt_llama_state_dict_keys(checkpoint, model_args)
433	
434	        original_default_type = torch.get_default_dtype()  # save the default type
435	        torch.set_default_tensor_type(torch.cuda.BFloat16Tensor)  # much faster loading
436	        model = LLaMA(model_args)
437	        model.load_state_dict(checkpoint, strict=False)
438	        torch.set_default_tensor_type(torch.tensor([], dtype=original_default_type, device="cpu").type())  # restore default type
439	
440	        tokenizer = Tokenizer(model_path=tokenizer_path)
441	        model.tokenizer = tokenizer
442	        return model
443	
444	    def configure_optimizers(self, weight_decay, learning_rate, betas, device_type, zero_stage):
445	        # start with all of the candidate parameters
446	        param_dict = {pn: p for pn, p in self.named_parameters()}
447	        # filter out those that do not require grad
448	        param_dict = {pn: p for pn, p in param_dict.items() if p.requires_grad}
449	        # create optim groups. Any parameters that is 2D will be weight decayed, otherwise no.
450	        # i.e. all weight tensors in matmuls + embeddings decay, all biases and layernorms don't.
451	        decay_params = [p for n, p in param_dict.items() if p.dim() >= 2]
452	        nodecay_params = [p for n, p in param_dict.items() if p.dim() < 2]
453	        optim_groups = [
454	            {'params': decay_params, 'weight_decay': weight_decay},
455	            {'params': nodecay_params, 'weight_decay': 0.0}
456	        ]
457	        num_decay_params = sum(p.numel() for p in decay_params)
458	        num_nodecay_params = sum(p.numel() for p in nodecay_params)
459	        print0(f"num decayed parameter tensors: {len(decay_params)}, with {num_decay_params:,} parameters")
460	        print0(f"num non-decayed parameter tensors: {len(nodecay_params)}, with {num_nodecay_params:,} parameters")
461	        # Create AdamW optimizer and use the fused version if it is available
462	        fused_available = 'fused' in inspect.signature(torch.optim.AdamW).parameters
463	        use_fused = fused_available and device_type == 'cuda'
464	        print0(f"using fused AdamW: {use_fused}")
465	        if zero_stage == 1:
466	            print0("using ZeroRedundancyOptimizer")
467	            optimizer = ZeroRedundancyOptimizer(**optim_groups[0], optimizer_class=torch.optim.AdamW,
468	                                                lr=learning_rate, betas=betas, fused=use_fused)
469	            optimizer.add_param_group(optim_groups[1])
470	        else:
471	            print0("using regular AdamW")
472	            optimizer = torch.optim.AdamW(optim_groups, lr=learning_rate, betas=betas, fused=use_fused)
473	        return optimizer
474	
475	    @torch.inference_mode()
476	    def generate(
477	        self,
478	        prompt_tokens: List[List[int]],
479	        max_gen_len: int,
480	        temperature: float = 0.6,
481	        top_p: float = 0.9,
482	        echo: bool = False,
483	    ) -> Tuple[List[List[int]], Optional[List[List[float]]]]:
484	        """
485	        Generate text sequences based on provided prompts using the language generation model.
486	
487	        Args:
488	            prompt_tokens (List[List[int]]): List of tokenized prompts, where each prompt is represented as a list of integers.
489	            max_gen_len (int): Maximum length of the generated text sequence.
490	            temperature (float, optional): Temperature value for controlling randomness in sampling. Defaults to 0.6.
491	            top_p (float, optional): Top-p probability threshold for nucleus sampling. Defaults to 0.9.
492	            echo (bool, optional): Flag indicating whether to include prompt tokens in the generated output. Defaults to False.
493	
494	        Returns:
495	            Tuple[List[List[int]], Optional[List[List[float]]]]: A tuple containing generated token sequences.
496	
497	        Note:
498	            This method uses the provided prompts as a basis for generating text. It employs nucleus sampling to produce text with controlled randomness.
499	
500	        """
501	        bsz = len(prompt_tokens)
502	        assert bsz <= self.config.max_gen_batch_size, f"Batch size {bsz} exceeds the maximum generation batch size {self.config.max_gen_batch_size}"
503	        device = next(self.parameters()).device
504	
505	        min_prompt_len = min(len(t) for t in prompt_tokens)
506	        max_prompt_len = max(len(t) for t in prompt_tokens)
507	        assert max_prompt_len <= self.config.block_size, f"Prompt length {max_prompt_len} exceeds the maximum block size {self.config.block_size}"
508	        total_len = min(self.config.block_size, max_gen_len + max_prompt_len)
509	
510	        pad_id = self.tokenizer.pad_id
511	        tokens = torch.full((bsz, total_len), pad_id, dtype=torch.long, device=device)
512	        for idx, t in enumerate(prompt_tokens):
513	            tokens[idx, : len(t)] = torch.tensor(t, dtype=torch.long, device=device)
514	
515	        prev_pos = 0
516	        eos_reached = torch.tensor([False] * bsz, device=device)
517	        input_text_mask = tokens != pad_id
518	
519	        if min_prompt_len == total_len:
520	            logits, _ = self.forward(tokens, start_pos=prev_pos)
521	
522	        stop_tokens = torch.tensor(list(self.tokenizer.stop_tokens)).to(device)
523	
524	        for cur_pos in range(min_prompt_len, total_len):
525	            logits, _ = self.forward(tokens[:, prev_pos:cur_pos], start_pos=prev_pos)
526	            if temperature > 0:
527	                probs = torch.softmax(logits[:, -1] / temperature, dim=-1)
528	                next_token = sample_top_p(probs, top_p)
529	            else:
530	                next_token = torch.argmax(logits[:, -1], dim=-1)
531	
532	            next_token = next_token.reshape(-1)
533	            # only replace token if prompt has already been generated
534	            next_token = torch.where(input_text_mask[:, cur_pos], tokens[:, cur_pos], next_token)
535	            tokens[:, cur_pos] = next_token
536	            eos_reached |= ~input_text_mask[:, cur_pos] & torch.isin(next_token, stop_tokens)
537	            prev_pos = cur_pos
538	            if all(eos_reached):
539	                break
540	
541	        out_tokens = []
542	        for i, toks in enumerate(tokens.tolist()):
543	            # cut to max gen len
544	            start = 0 if echo else len(prompt_tokens[i])
545	            toks = toks[start : len(prompt_tokens[i]) + max_gen_len]
546	            # cut to after eos tok if any
547	            for stop_token in self.tokenizer.stop_tokens:
548	                try:
549	                    eos_idx = toks.index(stop_token)
550	                    toks = toks[:eos_idx]
551	                except ValueError:
552	                    pass
553	            out_tokens.append(toks)
554	        return out_tokens
555	
556	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
557	# sampling utils
558	
559	def sample_top_p(probs, p):
560	    """
561	    Perform top-p (nucleus) sampling on a probability distribution.
562	
563	    Args:
564	        probs (torch.Tensor): Probability distribution tensor.
565	        p (float): Probability threshold for top-p sampling.
566	
567	    Returns:
568	        torch.Tensor: Sampled token indices.
569	
570	    Note:
571	        Top-p sampling selects the smallest set of tokens whose cumulative probability mass
572	        exceeds the threshold p. The distribution is renormalized based on the selected tokens.
573	    """
574	    probs_sort, probs_idx = torch.sort(probs, dim=-1, descending=True)
575	    probs_sum = torch.cumsum(probs_sort, dim=-1)
576	    mask = probs_sum - probs_sort > p
577	    probs_sort[mask] = 0.0
578	    probs_sort.div_(probs_sort.sum(dim=-1, keepdim=True))
579	    next_token = torch.multinomial(probs_sort, num_samples=1)
580	    next_token = torch.gather(probs_idx, -1, next_token)
581	    return next_token
582	
583	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
584	# Llama 3.1 Tokenizer
585	
586	# The tiktoken tokenizer can handle <=400k chars without
587	# pyo3_runtime.PanicException.
588	TIKTOKEN_MAX_ENCODE_CHARS = 400_000
589	
590	# https://github.com/openai/tiktoken/issues/195
591	# Here we iterate over subsequences and split if we exceed the limit
592	# of max consecutive non-whitespace or whitespace characters.
593	MAX_NO_WHITESPACES_CHARS = 25_000
594	
595	
596	class Tokenizer:
597	    """
598	    Tokenizing and encoding/decoding text using the Tiktoken tokenizer.
599	    """
600	
601	    special_tokens: Dict[str, int]
602	
603	    num_reserved_special_tokens = 256
604	
605	    pat_str = r"(?i:'s|'t|'re|'ve|'m|'ll|'d)|[^\r\n\p{L}\p{N}]?\p{L}+|\p{N}{1,3}| ?[^\s\p{L}\p{N}]+[\r\n]*|\s*[\r\n]+|\s+(?!\S)|\s+"  # noqa: E501
606	
607	    def __init__(self, model_path: str):
608	        """
609	        Initializes the Tokenizer with a Tiktoken model.
610	
611	        Args:
612	            model_path (str): The path to the Tiktoken model file.
613	        """
614	        assert os.path.isfile(model_path), model_path
615	
616	        mergeable_ranks = load_tiktoken_bpe(model_path)
617	        num_base_tokens = len(mergeable_ranks)
618	        special_tokens = [
619	            "<|begin_of_text|>",
620	            "<|end_of_text|>",
621	            "<|reserved_special_token_0|>",
622	            "<|reserved_special_token_1|>",
623	            "<|finetune_right_pad_id|>",
624	            "<|step_id|>",
625	            "<|start_header_id|>",
626	            "<|end_header_id|>",
627	            "<|eom_id|>",  # end of message
628	            "<|eot_id|>",  # end of turn
629	            "<|python_tag|>",
630	        ]
631	        reserved_tokens = [
632	            f"<|reserved_special_token_{2 + i}|>"
633	            for i in range(self.num_reserved_special_tokens - len(special_tokens))
634	        ]
635	        special_tokens = special_tokens + reserved_tokens
636	
637	        self.special_tokens = {
638	            token: num_base_tokens + i for i, token in enumerate(special_tokens)
639	        }
640	        self.model = tiktoken.Encoding(
641	            name=Path(model_path).name,
642	            pat_str=self.pat_str,
643	            mergeable_ranks=mergeable_ranks,
644	            special_tokens=self.special_tokens,
645	        )
646	
647	        self.n_words: int = num_base_tokens + len(special_tokens)
648	        # BOS / EOS token IDs
649	        self.bos_id: int = self.special_tokens["<|begin_of_text|>"]
650	        self.eos_id: int = self.special_tokens["<|end_of_text|>"]
651	        self.eot_id: int = self.special_tokens["<|eot_id|>"]
652	        self.eom_id: int = self.special_tokens["<|eom_id|>"]
653	        self.python_tag_id = self.special_tokens["<|python_tag|>"]
654	        self.pad_id: int = self.special_tokens["<|finetune_right_pad_id|>"]
655	        # hardcoded stop tokens for the base model
656	        self.stop_tokens = [
657	            self.special_tokens["<|begin_of_text|>"],
658	            self.special_tokens["<|end_of_text|>"],
659	        ]
660	
661	    def encode(
662	        self,
663	        s: str,
664	        *,
665	        bos: bool,
666	        eos: bool,
667	        allowed_special: Optional[Union[Literal["all"], AbstractSet[str]]] = None,
668	        disallowed_special: Union[Literal["all"], Collection[str]] = (),
669	    ) -> List[int]:
670	        """
671	        Encodes a string into a list of token IDs.
672	
673	        Args:
674	            s (str): The input string to be encoded.
675	            bos (bool): Whether to prepend the beginning-of-sequence token.
676	            eos (bool): Whether to append the end-of-sequence token.
677	            allowed_tokens ("all"|set[str]): allowed special tokens in string
678	            disallowed_tokens ("all"|set[str]): special tokens that raise an error when in string
679	
680	        Returns:
681	            list[int]: A list of token IDs.
682	
683	        By default, setting disallowed_special=() encodes a string by ignoring
684	        special tokens. Specifically:
685	        - Setting `disallowed_special` to () will cause all text corresponding
686	          to special tokens to be encoded as natural text (insteading of raising
687	          an error).
688	        - Setting `allowed_special` to "all" will treat all text corresponding
689	          to special tokens to be encoded as special tokens.
690	        """
691	        if allowed_special is None:
692	            allowed_special = set()
693	        assert type(s) is str
694	
695	        substrs = (
696	            substr
697	            for i in range(0, len(s), TIKTOKEN_MAX_ENCODE_CHARS)
698	            for substr in self._split_whitespaces_or_nonwhitespaces(
699	                s[i : i + TIKTOKEN_MAX_ENCODE_CHARS], MAX_NO_WHITESPACES_CHARS
700	            )
701	        )
702	        t: List[int] = []
703	        for substr in substrs:
704	            t.extend(
705	                self.model.encode(
706	                    substr,
707	                    allowed_special=allowed_special,
708	                    disallowed_special=disallowed_special,
709	                )
710	            )
711	        if bos:
712	            t.insert(0, self.bos_id)
713	        if eos:
714	            t.append(self.eos_id)
715	        return t
716	
717	    def decode(self, t: Sequence[int]) -> str:
718	        # Typecast is safe here. Tiktoken doesn't do anything list-related with the sequence.
719	        return self.model.decode(cast(List[int], t))
720	
721	    @staticmethod
722	    def _split_whitespaces_or_nonwhitespaces(
723	        s: str, max_consecutive_slice_len: int
724	    ) -> Iterator[str]:
725	        """
726	        Splits the string `s` so that each substring contains no more than `max_consecutive_slice_len`
727	        consecutive whitespaces or consecutive non-whitespaces.
728	        """
729	        current_slice_len = 0
730	        current_slice_is_space = s[0].isspace() if len(s) > 0 else False
731	        slice_start = 0
732	
733	        for i in range(len(s)):
734	            is_now_space = s[i].isspace()
735	
736	            if current_slice_is_space ^ is_now_space:
737	                current_slice_len = 1
738	                current_slice_is_space = is_now_space
739	            else:
740	                current_slice_len += 1
741	                if current_slice_len > max_consecutive_slice_len:
742	                    yield s[slice_start:i]
743	                    slice_start = i
744	                    current_slice_len = 1
745	        yield s[slice_start:]
746	
747	# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
748	# Our own simple Distributed Data Loader
749	
750	def _peek_data_shard(filename):
751	    # only reads the header, returns header data
752	    with open(filename, "rb") as f:
753	        # first read the header, which is 256 int32 integers (4 bytes each)
754	        header = np.frombuffer(f.read(256*4), dtype=np.int32)
755	    if header[0] != 20240801:
756	        print("ERROR: magic number mismatch in the data .bin file!")
757	        exit(1)
758	    assert header[1] == 7, "unsupported version"
759	    ntok = header[2] # number of tokens (claimed)
760	    return ntok # for now just return the number of tokens
761	
762	def _load_data_shard(filename):
763	    with open(filename, "rb") as f:
764	        # first read the header, which is 256 int32 integers (4 bytes each)
765	        header = np.frombuffer(f.read(256*4), dtype=np.int32)
766	        assert header[0] == 20240801, "magic number mismatch in the data .bin file"
767	        assert header[1] == 7, "unsupported version"
768	        ntok = header[2] # number of tokens (claimed)
769	        # the rest of it are tokens, stored as uint16
770	        tokens = np.frombuffer(f.read(), dtype=np.uint32)
771	    assert len(tokens) == ntok, "number of tokens read does not match header?"
772	    return tokens
773	
774	class DistributedShardedDataLoader:
775	    """
776	    This DataLoader is both:
777	    - distributed (works correctly in case of multiple processes in DDP)
778	    - sharded (supports datasets that are broken up into multiple data shards)
779	    It is not *permuted*, meaning that it itearates over the data in the order
780	    of the dataset on disk, so the user should make sure to shuffle their examples
781	    during the creation of their data shards for best performance.
782	    """
783	    def __init__(self, filename_pattern, B, T, process_rank, num_processes):
784	        self.process_rank = process_rank
785	        self.num_processes = num_processes
786	        self.B = B
787	        self.T = T
788	
789	        # glob files that match the pattern
790	        self.files = sorted(glob.glob(filename_pattern))
791	        assert len(self.files) > 0, f"did not find any files that match the pattern {filename_pattern}"
792	
793	        # load and validate all data shards, count number of tokens in total
794	        ntok_total = 0
795	        for fname in self.files:
796	            shard_ntok = _peek_data_shard(fname)
797	            assert shard_ntok >= num_processes * B * T + 1
798	            ntok_total += shard_ntok
799	        self.ntok_total = ntok_total
800	        print0(f"DataLoader: total number of tokens: {ntok_total:,} across {len(self.files)} files")
801	
802	        # kick things off
803	        self.current_shard = None
804	        self.reset()
805	
806	    def reset(self):
807	        # we're being a bit clever here: if we already had shard 0 loaded,
808	        # then don't do the work to reload it, just reset the pointer
809	        if self.current_shard != 0:
810	            self.current_shard = 0
811	            self.tokens = _load_data_shard(self.files[self.current_shard])
812	        self.current_position = self.process_rank * self.B * self.T
813	
814	    def advance(self): # advance to next data shard
815	        self.current_shard = (self.current_shard + 1) % len(self.files)
816	        self.current_position = self.process_rank * self.B * self.T
817	        self.tokens = _load_data_shard(self.files[self.current_shard])
818	
819	    def next_batch(self):
820	        B = self.B
821	        T = self.T
822	        buf = self.tokens[self.current_position : self.current_position+B*T+1]
823	        buf = torch.tensor(buf, dtype=torch.long)
824	        x = (buf[:-1]).view(B, T) # inputs
825	        y = (buf[1:]).view(B, T) # targets
826	        # advance the start pointer in current shard
827	        self.current_position += B * T * self.num_processes
828	        # if loading the next batch would be out of bounds advance the shard
829	        if self.current_position + (B * T * self.num_processes + 1) > len(self.tokens):
830	            self.advance()
831	        return x, y

834	# Python -> C bridge utilities for saving params/grads/activations to .bin files
835	
836	def write_fp32(tensor, file):
837	    t = tensor.detach().cpu().to(torch.float32)
838	    b = t.numpy().tobytes()
839	    file.write(b)
840	
841	def write_bf16(tensor, file):
842	    t = tensor.detach().cpu().to(torch.bfloat16)
843	    # numpy doesn't have bf16 datatype so we have to trick it
844	    t = t.view(torch.int16) # trick: reinterpret as int16
845	    b = t.numpy().tobytes()
846	    file.write(b)
847	
848	def write_tensors(model_tensors, L, file, dtype):
849	    # writes LLaMA 3 model's weights to a binary file
850	    assert dtype in {"float32", "bfloat16"}
851	    write_fun = write_fp32 if dtype == "float32" else write_bf16
852	    write_fun(model_tensors["transformer.wte.weight"], file) # (V, C)
853	    for i in range(L): # (L, C)
854	        write_fun(model_tensors[f"transformer.h.{i}.ln_1.weight"], file)
855	    for i in range(L): # (L, 3C, C)
856	        write_fun(model_tensors[f"transformer.h.{i}.attn.c_attn.weight"], file)
857	    for i in range(L): # (L, C, C)
858	        write_fun(model_tensors[f"transformer.h.{i}.attn.c_proj.weight"], file)
859	    for i in range(L): # (L, C)
860	        write_fun(model_tensors[f"transformer.h.{i}.ln_2.weight"], file)
861	    for i in range(L): # (L, 4C, C)
862	        write_fun(model_tensors[f"transformer.h.{i}.mlp.c_fc.weight"], file)
863	    for i in range(L): # (L, 4C, C)
864	        write_fun(model_tensors[f"transformer.h.{i}.mlp.c_fc2.weight"], file)
865	    for i in range(L): # (L, C, 4C)
866	        write_fun(model_tensors[f"transformer.h.{i}.mlp.c_proj.weight"], file)
867	    write_fun(model_tensors["transformer.ln_f.weight"], file) # (C, )
868	    write_fun(model_tensors["lm_head.weight"], file) # (V, C)
869	
870	def write_model(model, filename, dtype):
871	    # everything we need to instantiate the model
872	    # 1) header is: version int, LLaMAConfig ints, padding to 1024 bytes
873	    assert dtype in {"float32", "bfloat16"}
874	    version = {
875	        "float32": 3, # 3: all tensors are fp32
876	        "bfloat16": 5, # 5: all tensors are bf16
877	    }[dtype]
878	    header = torch.zeros(256, dtype=torch.int32)
879	    header[0] = 20240803 # magic
880	    header[1] = version # checkpoint version
881	    header[2] = model.config.block_size
882	    header[3] = model.config.vocab_size
883	    header[4] = model.config.n_layer
884	    header[5] = model.config.n_head
885	    header[6] = model.config.n_kv_head
886	    header[7] = model.config.n_embd
887	    header[8] = model.config.ffn_dim_multiplier
888	    header[9] = model.config.multiple_of
889	    header[10] = model.config.norm_eps
890	    header[11] = model.config.rope_theta
891	    header[12] = model.config.use_scaled_rope
892	    header[13] = model.config.max_gen_batch_size
893	    header[14] = int(model.config.version.split('.')[0]) # major version
894	    header[15] = int(model.config.version.split('.')[1]) # minor version
895	    # 2) the parameters follow the header
896	    params = {name: param.cpu() for name, param in model.named_parameters()}
897	    # now write to file
898	    with open(filename, "wb") as file:
899	        file.write(header.numpy().tobytes()) # header
900	        write_tensors(params, model.config.n_layer, file, dtype) # params
901	    print(f"wrote {filename}")
902	
903	def write_state(model, x, y, logits, loss, filename):
904	    # the state is used for debugging.
905	    # it contains information about the input, logits, loss, and the parameter gradients
906	    # this can be used for checking the computation correctness in C
907	    header = torch.zeros(256, dtype=torch.int32)
908	    header[0] = 20240803 # magic
909	    header[1] = x.size(0) # batch size of the batch, B
910	    header[2] = x.size(1) # temporal extent of the batch, T
911	    grads = {name: param.grad.cpu() for name, param in model.named_parameters()}
912	    with open(filename, "wb") as file:
913	        # header
914	        file.write(header.numpy().tobytes())
915	        # input x
916	        file.write(x.cpu().numpy().astype("int32").tobytes()) # (B, T)
917	        # targets y
918	        file.write(y.cpu().numpy().astype("int32").tobytes()) # (B, T)
919	        # logits (result of the model forward pass)
920	        write_fp32(logits.cpu(), file)
921	        # loss (single float, result of the cross entropy loss)
922	        write_fp32(loss.cpu(), file)
923	        # gradients
924	        write_tensors(grads, model.config.n_layer, file, "float32")
925	    print(f"wrote {filename}")

1179	        # bit confusing: we want to make sure to eval and sample on 0th iteration
1180	        # but also after the very last iteration. so we loop for step <= num_iterations
1181	        # instead of just < num_iterations (one extra due to <=), only to do
1182	        # the validation/sampling one last time, and then we break right here as we're done.
1183	        if last_step:
1184	            break
1185	
1186	        # --------------- TRAINING SECTION BEGIN -----------------
1187	        model.train()
1188	        optimizer.zero_grad(set_to_none=True)
1189	        # if we are trying to overfit a single batch, we reset the loader here
1190	        if args.overfit_single_batch:
1191	            train_loader.reset()
1192	        # micro-batch loop where we do gradient accumulation to reach desired total batch size
1193	        lossf = 0.0 # for getting the mean loss (as simple float) over the accumulation steps
1194	        for micro_step in range(grad_accum_steps):
1195	            # fetch a batch
1196	            x, y = train_loader.next_batch()
1197	            x, y = x.to(device), y.to(device)
1198	            if ddp:
1199	                # we want only the last micro-step to sync grads in a DDP model
1200	                # the official way to do this is with model.no_sync(), but that is a
1201	                # context manager that bloats the code, so we just toggle this variable
1202	                model.require_backward_grad_sync = (micro_step == grad_accum_steps - 1)
1203	            # forward pass
1204	            with ctx:
1205	                _, loss = model(x, y, return_logits=False)
1206	                # we have to scale the loss to account for gradient accumulation,
1207	                # because the gradients just add on each successive backward().
1208	                # addition of gradients corresponds to a SUM in the objective, but
1209	                # instead of a SUM we want MEAN, so we scale the loss here
1210	                loss = loss / grad_accum_steps
1211	                lossf += loss.detach() # keep track of the mean loss
1212	            # backward pass
1213	            if not args.inference_only:
1214	                loss.backward()
1215	        if ddp:
1216	            dist.all_reduce(lossf, op=dist.ReduceOp.AVG)
1217	        lossf = lossf.item()
1218	        norm = torch.nn.utils.clip_grad_norm_(model.parameters(), args.grad_clip)
1219	        # determine and set the learning rate for this iteration
1220	        lr = get_lr(step)
1221	        for param_group in optimizer.param_groups:
1222	            param_group['lr'] = lr
1223	        # step the optimizer
1224	        optimizer.step()
1225	        # --------------- TRAINING SECTION END -------------------
1226	        # everything that follows now is just diagnostics, prints, logging, etc.
1227	
1228	        # wait on the CPU for all device work to end so we get accurate per-iteration timings below
1229	        if device == "mps":
1230	            torch.mps.synchronize()
1231	        elif device == "cuda":
1232	            torch.cuda.synchronize()
1233	        # time and print
1234	        t1 = time.time()
1235	        # the 0th iteration is often an outlier (much slower) => skip logging it
1236	        tokens_per_second = grad_accum_steps * ddp_world_size * B * T / (t1-t0)
1237	        print0(f"step {step+1:4d}/{args.num_iterations} | train loss {lossf:.6f} | norm {norm:.4f} | lr {lr:.2e} | ({(t1-t0)*1000:.2f} ms | {tokens_per_second:.0f} tok/s)")
1238	        # log to logile
1239	        if master_process and logfile is not None:
1240	            with open(logfile, "a") as f:
1241	                f.write("s:%d trl:%f\n" % (step, lossf))
1242	
1243	        # keep track of smooth timings, last 20 iterations
1244	        if step > 0 and step > args.num_iterations - 20:
1245	            timings.append(t1-t0)
1246	
1247	    # print the average of the last 20 timings, to get something smooth-ish
1248	    timings = timings[-20:]
1249	    print0(f"final {len(timings)} iters avg: {np.mean(timings)*1000:.3f}ms")
1250	    print0(f"peak memory consumption: {torch.cuda.max_memory_allocated() // 1024 // 1024} MiB")
1251	
1252	    # -------------------------------------------------------------------------
1253	    # clean up nice
1254	    if ddp:
1255	        destroy_process_group()
